# Exploring the He-Normal Distribution in Neural Network Weight Initialization

## Introduction

Everton Gomede, PhD

Follow

5 min read

·

Jan 11, 2024

2

Listen

Share

The evolution of neural network architectures has led to the development of various strategies to optimize their performance. Among these, the choice of weight initialization plays a crucial role. The He-normal distribution, introduced by Kaiming He et al., in their 2015 paper, is a significant advancement in this area, particularly for networks employing Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) activations. This essay explores the concept, significance, and impact of the He-normal distribution in the initialization of neural network weights.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

> In the world of neural networks, the key to unlocking potential lies not just in the architecture, but in the very seeds we sow. The He-normal distribution, in its meticulous calibration of weight initialization, serves as a reminder that even the most complex journeys begin with a single, well-placed step.
> 
> 

### Background: The Challenge of Weight Initialization

Before delving into the He-normal distribution, it is important to understand the problem it addresses. Neural networks, especially deep ones, often suffer from issues like vanishing or exploding gradients during training. Such problems are significantly influenced by the way weights are initialized.
